content:
Major units of basics and sediments on the western margins of both
Cuddingwarra and Wattagee are thought to form part of the west limb of a
tight northeast plunging antiform
(interpretation based on regional mag-
netic and geological data - W.A.G.S.) with granitic rocks explaced along
the axial region and lying marginal to the Cuddingwarra basics.
The
Big Bell gold mine,which is hosted by sediments,
